name: Professional Services and Consultancy Procurement
description: This Direction establishes the framework for procuring professional services, including consultants, by NSW government agencies. It applies to all temporary engagements of external individuals or companies to provide expertise, experience, and specialised knowledge. Consultancy is a subset of professional services where the primary deliverable is advice or recommendations. The scheme requires agencies to justify consultant use, minimise duration, ensure value for money and probity, manage conflicts of interest, assess performance, and comply with Core Work policy restrictions.
source: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912 (as amended); NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework; Procurement Board Direction PBD-2026-02 — Engagement of Professional Services Suppliers (April 2026; supersedes PBD-2023-06); Core NSW Public Service Work Policy; Whole of Government Core Work Circular (C2025-05); Government Sector Finance Act 2018

pre_checks.title: Define the need for consultant versus other resource types
pre_checks.body: Before engaging a consultant, clearly define why a consultant (rather than permanent staff, other contractors, or internal expertise) is required for the work. Document the justification for engaging external resources.

pre_checks.title: Classify the engagement as consultancy or non-consultancy
pre_checks.body: Determine whether the engagement is predominantly consultancy (advice/recommendations), non-consultancy (implementation/delivery), or a mix. Base classification on the predominant component by value. Indicate the classification in the contract.
pre_checks.citation: Definitions of Professional Services and Consultancy section

pre_checks.title: Check Core Work policy alignment
pre_checks.body: Review the Core NSW Public Service Work Policy to determine whether any element of the work must be performed by government employees or must not be outsourced to consultants. Consider sourcing expertise internally via the Expert Advisory Network (EAN) before engaging external consultants.
pre_checks.citation: Reducing over-reliance on external resources and leveraging government internal expertise section

pre_checks.title: Identify and manage conflicts of interest
pre_checks.body: Identify potential, perceived, or actual conflicts of interest before and throughout the engagement. Document all conflicts identified and the management approach applied.

pre_checks.title: Check applicable whole-of-government arrangements
pre_checks.body: Determine whether a whole-of-government professional services procurement arrangement exists that covers the required services (e.g., Performance and Management Services Scheme SCM0005, ICT Services Scheme, Legal Services Panel, Built Heritage Conservation Consultants Scheme, etc.). Use the arrangement if available. Required under PBD-2026-02 (supersedes PBD-2023-06).

obligations.title: Define need for consultant engagement
obligations.body: The need to use a consultant, rather than other resource types (such as permanent employees or alternative service delivery models), must be clearly defined before engagement.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Minimise consultant engagement duration
obligations.body: The duration of the consultancy engagement must be as short as possible while still enabling the consultant to undertake the required work. Long-term or indefinite engagements must be justified.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Justify all variations to engagement contracts
obligations.body: Any variations to an engagement contract must have a clear and specific justification documented prior to approving the variation.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Apply confidentiality provisions
obligations.body: The engagement must be subject to appropriate confidentiality provisions that protect sensitive government information and comply with NSW privacy and information management requirements.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Declare and manage conflicts of interest
obligations.body: All conflicts of interest (potential, perceived, or actual) must be declared by the consultant prior to and monitored throughout the engagement. A documented conflict management process must be in place.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Assess consultant work performance
obligations.body: An assessment of the consultant's work must be performed during or at the conclusion of the engagement to evaluate quality, outcomes, and value for money delivered.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Maintain appropriate documentation and records
obligations.body: Comprehensive documentation and record keeping practices must be maintained for all consultant engagements, including contract terms, performance assessments, payments, and variations.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Clearly identify consultants in government systems
obligations.body: Consultants must be clearly identified as external contractors in government systems including e-mail systems, security passes, staff directories, and physical workspaces to ensure transparency and prevent confusion with permanent employees.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Restrict consultant decision-making authority in recruitment
obligations.body: Consultants must not have decision-making authority in recruitment or selection processes for other consultants and contractors. Consultants may participate in recruitment activities only in a supporting or advisory capacity.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

obligations.title: Classify engagement in contract
obligations.body: Every professional services engagement contract must clearly indicate the classification of the engagement as consultancy (advice), non-consultancy (delivery/implementation), or a mix thereof, based on the predominant component by value.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework
obligations.citation: Definitions of Professional Services and Consultancy section

obligations.title: Report consultant engagement data to Procurement Board
obligations.body: All agencies are required to report 'consultant' engagement data to the Procurement Board through the Principal Department of their affiliated agency group. Data must include supplier name, ABN, applicable procurement arrangement, contract reference, estimated value, engagement dates, classification (consultancy/non-consultancy/mix), cost centre, core work assessment, and reason for outsourcing. Inclusion of non-consultant professional services data is recommended.
obligations.policy: Government Sector Finance Act 2018; Public Works and Procurement Act 1912
obligations.citation: Agency reporting requirements section; Table 1: Consultant Data Collection Field Requirements

obligations.title: Report annual consultant expenditure
obligations.body: All agencies are required to report 'consultant' expenditure in accordance with Annual Report requirements under the Government Sector Finance Act 2018. Internal government advice (including EAN sourced advice) and NSW Government Legal Services Panel services are excluded from reporting.

obligations.title: Whole-of-Government scheme owners report annually
obligations.body: Responsible agencies of whole-of-government professional services schemes must report annually to the Procurement Board and Minister for Domestic Manufacturing and Government Procurement on use of the arrangement, including comparative analysis across agency portfolios on engagement numbers, total spend, compliance with scheme requirements, and trends.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework
obligations.citation: Whole-of-Government procurement arrangement owner reporting requirements section

obligations.title: Apply Core Work policy requirements
obligations.body: Agencies must comply with the Core NSW Public Service Work Policy which defines types of work that must be performed by government employees and types that must not be outsourced to consultants. Consider internal capability via the Expert Advisory Network before engaging external consultants.
obligations.policy: Core NSW Public Service Work Policy; Whole of Government Core Work Circular (C2025-05)
obligations.citation: Reducing over-reliance on external resources and leveraging government internal expertise section

obligations.title: Ensure value for money, probity, and transparency
obligations.body: All professional services engagements must be procured with value for money, probity, and transparency as key objectives. The engagement process must be transparent and the selection rationale must be clearly documented.
obligations.policy: Public Works and Procurement Act 1912; NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework

pathway_overrides.if_arrangement_exists: Use the applicable whole-of-government professional services procurement arrangement (e.g., Performance and Management Services Scheme SCM0005, ICT Services Scheme SCM0020, Consultants in Construction Scheme SCM1191, Government Architect's Strategy and Design Scheme SCM0801, Legal Services Panel, or Built Heritage Conservation Consultants Scheme SCM3831) in accordance with that arrangement's specific requirements.
pathway_overrides.if_no_arrangement: Conduct open market procurement for the required professional services in accordance with the Public Works and Procurement Act 1912 and NSW Government Procurement Policy Framework, ensuring compliance with all obligations in this Direction including conflict of interest management, Core Work policy review, and data collection requirements.

steps.text: Determine whether the engagement is for consultancy (advice/recommendations) or non-consultancy professional services (implementation/delivery) based on the predominant component. If mixed, classify by the predominant value element.
steps.citation: Definitions of Professional Services and Consultancy section

steps.text: Review the Core NSW Public Service Work Policy to confirm that the proposed work is not classified as work that must be performed by government employees or must not be outsourced.
steps.citation: Reducing over-reliance on external resources and leveraging government internal expertise section

steps.text: Check whether the required services are covered by an existing whole-of-government professional services procurement arrangement. If available, proceed via that arrangement.
Identify and document potential, perceived, and actual conflicts of interest that the consultant may have. Establish a conflict management approach.
Define and justify the specific need for external consultant engagement rather than using permanent staff or other resource types.
Establish the minimum engagement duration required to complete the work; longer durations require documented justification.

approval_additions.note: All consultant engagements must be reported to the Procurement Board through the Principal Department of the affiliated agency group as required by Government Sector Finance Act 2018
Engagement must be assessed against the Core NSW Public Service Work Policy to confirm the work is not restricted from outsourcing
Consultant conflicts of interest (potential, perceived, actual) must be identified and managed prior to engagement commencement
Engagement contract must include appropriate confidentiality provisions and be reviewed for compliance with procurement probity and transparency requirements
